title: Xerophilous Fried Vietnamese Bacon Goi Cuon
description: A savory, flaky, and festive Vietnamese dish featuring crispy bacon lardon, perfect for slow-cooked vegetarian delights. This goi cuon recipe is a unique twist on the traditional dish, featuring a slate-tile vessel.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon of sesame oil over medium heat. Add the bacon lardon and cook until crispy, about 6 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove the bacon from the skillet and set aside on a paper towel-lined plate.
In the same skillet, add the shredded daikon and carrots and cook until tender, about 4 minutes, stirring occasionally.
In a separate pan or wok, heat 2 tablespoons of sesame oil over high heat. Add the pickled carrots and cook until caramelized, about 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Meanwhile, prepare the rice paper wrappers by soaking them in warm water for about 10 minutes.
To assemble the goi cuon, lay a sheet of rice paper on a clean surface and arrange a few pieces of the cooked daikon and carrots in the center.
Add a few slices of the crispy bacon, some chopped cilantro, and a lime wedge on top. Repeat with the remaining ingredients.
Serve the goi cuon on a slate-tile vessel with the fried shallots and toasted peanuts on the side, accompanied by a side of Vietnamese dipping sauce for serving.
Refrigerate the goi cuon for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together before serving.
Cook the goi cuon in a deep frying pan with about 1-2 inches of vegetable oil over medium-high heat until golden brown and crispy, about 3-4 minutes on each side, flipping occasionally.
Drain the goi cuon on paper towels and serve hot.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
